title: Seriphium prostratum
taxonomy: Seriphium prostratum is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the genus Seriphium, which contains a small number of species of flowering plants. Seriphium prostratum is native to the Mediterranean region and has been introduced to other parts of the world. This species is a type of evergreen shrub that is low-growing and spreading, making it a popular choice for landscaping. The classification of Seriphium prostratum also places it within the order Asterales.

intro: Seriphium prostratum, also known as creeping seriphium, is a low-growing, spreading shrub native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, inflammation, and fever, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Seriphium prostratum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
